{"query":"Julian Assange 14-year legal saga conclusion date outcome","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1794,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://abcnews.com/US/timeline-julian-assanges-14-year-long-saga-dodge/story?id=111398254","title":"Timeline of Julian Assange's 14-year-long saga to dodge extradition - ABC News","content":"The WikiLeaks founder agreed to a no-jail plea deal with U.S. prosecutors.
Under the plea deal, the 52-year-old Australian former computer programmer was sentenced Wednesday by a judge in the Northern Mariana Islands to time served and set free.
June 24, 2024 -- Assange pleads guilty to a single felony count of conspiring to unlawfully obtain classified information and is sentenced to time served."},{"url":"https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/timeline-of-the-julian-assange-legal-saga-as-he-makes-a-final-bid-to-avoid-extradition-to-the-u-s","title":"Timeline of the Julian Assange legal saga as he makes a final bid to avoid extradition to the U.S. | PBS News","content":"Timeline of the Julian Assange legal saga as he makes a final bid to avoid extradition to the U.S.
LONDON (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange has been fighting for more than a decade to avoid extradition to the United States to face charges related to his organization's publication of a huge trove of classified documents.
Metropolitan Police end their 24-hour guard outside the Ecuadorian embassy but say they'll arrest Assange if he leaves, ending a three-year police operation estimated to have cost millions.
A U.S. court filing that appears to inadvertently reveal the existence of a sealed criminal case against Assange is discovered by a researcher."},{"url":"https://www.npr.org/2024/06/25/nx-s1-5019590/julian-assange-pleads-guilty","title":"WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange is free, ending years-long legal saga : NPR","content":"WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange is free, ending years-long legal saga Assange pleaded guilty to a felony charge of violating the Espionage Act.
WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange arrives at the United States Courthouse where he entered a guilty plea to an espionage charge.
Assange pleaded guilty to a single count of conspiring to obtain and disclose information related to the national defense in a U.S. federal court.
The indictment accused Assange of conspiring with then-military Private Chelsea Manning to obtain and then publish secret reports about the Afghanistan and Iraq wars and sensitive U.S. diplomatic cables.
Manning was arrested in 2010 and served seven years in prison before President Barack Obama commuted her sentence."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cw44l170xdwo","title":"Julian Assange: Wikileaks founder can challenge US extradition","content":"The 52-year-old’s lawyers hugged each other in court after the latest ruling in the legal saga.
Earlier on Monday, his wife Stella Assange told the BBC that she would "fight on until Julian is free", whatever the judges ruled."},{"url":"https://apnews.com/article/julian-assange-wikileaks-timeline-18088045fb2d5dbd1cdd9f3821b35a53","title":"A timeline of the legal case involving WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange | AP News","content":"WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange has returned to his homeland Australia aboard a charter jet hours after pleading guilty to obtaining and publishing U.S. military secrets.
The drawn-out legal case involving WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange has concluded with his guilty plea to obtaining and publishing U.S. military secrets.
Justice Department, Assange was sentenced Wednesday to the five years he’d already served in a British prison while fighting extradition.
20, 2024: Assange’s lawyers launch a final legal bid to stop his extradition at the High Court.
The date of the hearing has yet to be determined.
June 26, 2024: Assange pleads guilty to an Espionage Act charge of conspiring to unlawfully obtain and disseminate classified national defense information."}]}

His court hearing was held in Saipan, in the Northern Mariana Islands, a U.S. commonwealth.**. SAIPAN, Northern Mariana Islands — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange walked out of court a free man Wednesday after a hours-long court appearance in which he pleaded guilty to a felony charge of violating the Espionage Act. Assange, 52, is best known for the publication of classified military and diplomatic cables in 2010. His hearing was held in federal court in the Northern Mariana Islands, a U.S. commonwealth. The proceedings ended a years-long legal saga involving the WikiLeaks founder who spent years holed up in the Ecuadorian embassy in London before being imprisoned in the U.K. He is expected to return to his native Australia after the proceedings.","score":0.77446437,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/2024/06/24/us/julian-assange-wikileaks-timeline.html","title":"A Timeline of Julian Assange's Legal Saga","content":"A plea deal brought an abrupt end to an extraordinary legal saga that has raised novel issues of national security, press freedoms, politics and diplomacy.","score":0.7077487,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://abcnews.com/US/timeline-julian-assanges-14-year-long-saga-dodge/story?id=111398254","title":"Timeline of Julian Assange's 14-year-long saga to dodge ...","content":"ending his 14-year-long fight to avoid extradition to the United States to face prosecution.
